Checking whether the inferential steps hold is central to any meaningful 'check' of this artifact.","R1.3":"Load-bearing. The paper's sole claim—'VisionPoLL better reflects human preferences than single-judge baselines'—is never directly evidenced. Fleiss' kappa measures inter-machine-judge agreement; variance reduction measures score stability; Spearman re-run correlation measures procedural reproducibility. No human preference correlation, MTurk study, Bradley-Terry fit, or equivalent is reported. The evidence-claim gap is the primary failure mode of this artifact."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this check be limited to whether the statistical evidence logically supports the human-alignment claim (validity/soundness only), or do you also want coverage of writing quality, novelty against prior work, and citation completeness?"}

rationale.R1.2: The logical chain from the three reported metrics (Fleiss' kappa, variance reduction, Spearman re-run correlation) to the central conclusion of human-preference superiority is structurally invalid: none of the metrics measure correspondence with human judgments. Checking whether the inferential steps hold is central to any meaningful 'check' of this artifact.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing. The paper's sole claim—'VisionPoLL better reflects human preferences than single-judge baselines'—is never directly evidenced. Fleiss' kappa measures inter-machine-judge agreement; variance reduction measures score stability; Spearman re-run correlation measures procedural reproducibility. No human preference correlation, MTurk study, Bradley-Terry fit, or equivalent is reported. The evidence-claim gap is the primary failure mode of this artifact.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this check be limited to whether the statistical evidence logically supports the human-alignment claim (validity/soundness only), or do you also want coverage of writing quality, novelty against prior work, and citation completeness?
